name: Peñalara Circular Trail
name_original: Laguna de los Pájaros, Risco de los Claveles y Pico de Peñalara — circular desde Puerto de Cotos
description: Hike a challenging 8.0-mile circular route from Cotos Port to Peñalara Peak, Bird Lagoon, and the Cliff of the Carnations.
region_intro: Hiking around Real Sitio De San Ildefonso offers diverse landscapes at the foot of the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains. The region features the Montes de Valsaín, known for its rich nature and forestry, and the Eresma River, which forms the historic Pesquerías Reales. Hikers will encounter varied terrain, including wooded areas, rugged sections, and significant elevation changes, particularly in the higher reaches of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. Best hiking trails around Real Sitio De San Ildefonso The most popular hiking route is Peñalara Circular Trail, a 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the high-altitude terrain around Peñalara. Another top favourite among local hikers is Siete Picos Trail, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.4 km) path. This trail offers views across the Sierra de Guadarrama, traversing several peaks. Local hikers also love the Guadarrama National Park - circular to Peñalara from Cotos via the Peñalara Glacial Circus, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, often completed in about 4 hours 29 minutes. Hiking around Real Sitio De San Ildefonso is defined by the high peaks of the Sierra de Guadarrama, the forested slopes of Montes de Valsaín, and the historic river paths along the Eresma River. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Real Sitio De San Ildefonso are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 1,500 reviews. More than 12,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Real Sitio De San Ildefonso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Real Sitio De San Ildefonso?
A: The Real Sitio de San Ildefonso area offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 230 routes recorded on komoot. These range from easy strolls along riverbanks to challenging mountain ascents within the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, there are plenty of easy options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1148123207/valsain-blue-itinerary-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valsaín Blue Itinerary Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7 km route. The &lt;p&gt;&lt;em&gt;Rio Eresma from Boca del Asno&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/p&gt; is also noted as an easy, family-friendly circular hike within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hikers can explore diverse natural beauty, from the rich forests of the &lt;strong&gt;Montes de Valsaín&lt;/strong&gt; to the historic banks of the &lt;strong&gt;Eresma River&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its Pesquerías Reales. In the higher altitudes, you'll find dramatic landscapes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3692534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalara Glacial Cirque&lt;/a&gt; and the peaks of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/121253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seven Peaks&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Maliciosa Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region offers routes that include waterfalls. The &lt;em&gt;Hielo Pulmones Waterfall loop from Cotos&lt;/em&gt; is an 11.1 km mountaineering route that takes you to a notable waterfall within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Real Sitio De San Ildefonso?
A: Absolutely. Many trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1229518883/penalara-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalara Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging high-altitude loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957192404/guadarrama-national-park-circular-to-penalara-from-cotos-via-the-penalara-glacial-circus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadarrama National Park - circular to Peñalara from Cotos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1230965430/trail-of-the-royal-sites" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Royal Sites&lt;/a&gt; connects La Granja, Valsaín, and La Pradera de Navalhorno, tracing the historic Pesquerías Reales. You can also explore the extensive gardens of the &lt;strong&gt;Royal Palace of La Granja&lt;/strong&gt;, a masterpiece of 18th-century landscaping.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Real Sitio De San Ildefonso?
A: The trails in Real Sitio De San Ildefonso are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 1,500 reviews. More than 12,000 hikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the stunning mountain views and the well-preserved natural environment.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced hikers and mountaineers?
A: Definitely. The Sierra de Guadarrama National Park offers demanding ascents, such as the routes to &lt;strong&gt;Pico Peñalara&lt;/strong&gt;, the highest peak in the range. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1147887464/siete-picos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Siete Picos Trail&lt;/a&gt; also provides a moderate yet rewarding challenge with significant elevation gains and panoramic views.

Q: Are there trails with good viewpoints in the area?
A: Many trails offer spectacular vistas, especially those in the Sierra de Guadarrama. Peaks like &lt;strong&gt;La Maliciosa&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Siete Picos&lt;/strong&gt; provide expansive views. You can also visit specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5811468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Gitana&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic scenery.

Q: Is it possible to reach trailheads using public transport?
A: While specific public transport details for every trailhead can vary, the region is generally accessible. Key areas like Cotos Pass, a common starting point for many Sierra de Guadarrama routes, often have bus connections from nearby towns. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Real Sitio De San Ildefonso?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on most hiking trails in the Real Sitio de San Ildefonso and Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, especially if kept on a leash. However, specific rules may apply in certain protected areas or during particular seasons, so it's always best to check local signage or park regulations beforehand.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in this region?
A: The Real Sitio de San Ildefonso offers hiking opportunities year-round. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with wildflowers in spring and rich foliage in autumn. Summer is ideal for higher-altitude trails in the Sierra de Guadarrama, while winter offers unique snowshoeing or winter hiking experiences, particularly around Cotos Pass, though proper gear is essential.
tips: On the slope to the left appears the Cubero spring, dry or frozen for part of the year, and shortly after, where the mountain track turns to the left, the La Gitana viewpoint appears, with a giant compass to mark the hand with the iron needle the outstanding peaks of the landscape in the circle of the metallic compass. On one side of the viewpoint there is a strange sundial built with granite blocks and a poem by Federico García Lorca taken from the gypsy ballads and chiselled into the rocks. The verses say how many times will she wait / fresh face, black hair / on this green railing, and it must be because of the gypsy who gives her name to the mountain balcony. The excursion passes the viewpoint, leaves the main track and turns right onto a path that begins at the steps that come out of the building that has information panels on the routes, park regulations, advice and recommendations and serves as protection in case of rain or blizzard

The path jumps over a rocky hill and reaches the bridge over the Laguna de Peñalara stream, which is the natural drainage of the Laguna Grande. The Hoya de Peñalara glacial cirque shows all its reliefs between the peaks of Peñalara peak (2,428 m) and Dos Hermanas (2,284 m). The bites of the old moraines have left large granite steps, walls, channels, spurs, valleys and niches where lagoons and wetlands are formed.
If you get to the top of Peñalara, it is well worth visiting the Laguna Grande located within the picturesque Peñalara Circus. A wonderful and quiet place with nature in all its splendor.

Laguna de los Pájaros is a beautiful glacial lagoon located on the northeast slope of Peñalara Peak, in the Sierra de Guadarrama, within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. It is located at an altitude of 2,170 meters above sea level, making it the highest lagoon in the park.
During the winter, the lagoon remains frozen, and in summer, although its level fluctuates, it does not disappear completely. It is a popular place for hiking, with routes that offer spectacular views of the surrounding alpine landscape.
Small secondary peak of Penalara. A bit of climbing is required from the north-east side.
It is one of the most emblematic peaks of this mountain system and its summit is the highest point in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

faqs.question: Is this circular hike suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this hike is rated as difficult and is not suitable for beginners. It requires very good physical fitness, sturdy shoes, and alpine experience. Parts of the route are technical, difficult, or hazardous, sometimes requiring the use of hands and specialist equipment.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies significantly. You'll encounter mountain hiking paths, rocky ridges, and some unpaved or gravel sections. The segment from Peñalara Peak to Lake of the Birds, especially when traversing the rocky ridge to Claveles Crag, is particularly challenging, rocky, and not always well-marked, with drops on both sides.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers stunning and varied scenery. You'll pass by the Mirador de la Gitana, the impressive Peñalara Glacial Cirque, and glacial lakes like Laguna Grande de Peñalara and Lake of the Birds. The hike also takes you to the summits of Claveles Crag and Peñalara Peak, offering panoramic views of the Sierra de Guadarrama and beyond.

faqs.question: How do I get to the trailhead at Cotos Port, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The trailhead is located at Cotos Port (Puerto de Cotos). It is accessible by car, and parking is available there. You can also reach Cotos Port by intercity bus from Madrid, making it convenient for those using public transport.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Peñalara Natural Park, specific regulations may apply to certain areas, especially sensitive ecological zones or during certain seasons. It's always best to check the latest park rules regarding dogs, and they should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically from late spring to early autumn (June to October) when the weather is more stable and snow has melted from the higher elevations. Winter conditions can make the trail significantly more challenging and require specialized equipment like crampons and ice axes.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for Peñalara Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There is generally no entrance fee to access the Peñalara Natural Park itself. However, certain activities or access to specific sensitive areas might require permits or have restrictions. It's advisable to check the official website of the Parque Natural de la Cumbre, el Circo y las Lagunas de Peñalara for any current regulations or permit requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Puerto de Cotos - La Granja, Laguna de los Pájaros, and Pico de Peñalara trails. It also briefly touches upon the Circuito Peñalara and Laguna de Peñalara routes.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging hike?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult rating and varied terrain, you should pack sturdy hiking boots, appropriate layers of clothing for changing mountain weather, plenty of water, high-energy snacks, a map and compass (or GPS device), and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles can be very helpful, especially on the rocky and steep sections. Consider a headlamp if starting early or finishing late.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near Cotos Port?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cotos Port is a popular starting point, and you can usually find some basic amenities there, including a small cafe or refuge. For more extensive options like pubs, restaurants, and accommodation, you would typically need to venture to nearby villages such as Rascafría or La Granja de San Ildefonso.

faqs.question: What makes the ridge section to Claveles Crag particularly challenging?
faqs.answer: The section from Peñalara Peak to Claveles Crag is a rocky ridge that is not always well-marked. It often requires the use of hands for balance and scrambling, with significant drops on both sides. This part demands excellent sure-footedness and a good head for heights.
